comment_text.sn35.146:1.4: That is to say, the fact that the eye exists is because we were born into this life driven by the deeds in past lives.

root_text.sn35.146:1.1: “Navapurāṇāni, bhikkhave, kammāni desessāmi kammanirodhaṁ kammanirodhagāminiñca paṭipadaṁ.
root_text.sn35.146:1.2: Taṁ suṇātha, sādhukaṁ manasi karotha, bhāsissāmīti.
root_text.sn35.146:1.4: Cakkhu, bhikkhave, purāṇakammaṁ abhisaṅkhataṁ abhisañcetayitaṁ vedaniyaṁ daṭṭhabbaṁ …pe…
root_text.sn35.146:1.5: jivhā purāṇakammā abhisaṅkhatā abhisañcetayitā vedaniyā daṭṭhabbā …pe…
root_text.sn35.146:1.6: mano purāṇakammo abhisaṅkhato abhisañcetayito vedaniyo daṭṭhabbo.
root_text.sn35.146:1.9: Yaṁ kho, bhikkhave, etarahi kammaṁ karoti kāyena vācāya manasā,
root_text.sn35.146:1.12: Yo kho, bhikkhave, kāyakammavacīkammamanokammassa nirodhā vimuttiṁ phusati,
root_text.sn35.146:1.16: sammādiṭṭhi, sammāsaṅkappo, sammāvācā, sammākammanto, sammāājīvo, sammāvāyāmo, sammāsati, sammāsamādhi—
root_text.sn35.146:1.18: Iti kho, bhikkhave, desitaṁ mayā purāṇakammaṁ, desitaṁ navakammaṁ, desito kammanirodho, desitā kammanirodhagāminī paṭipadā.
root_text.sn35.146:1.19: Yaṁ kho, bhikkhave, satthārā karaṇīyaṁ sāvakānaṁ hitesinā anukampakena anukampaṁ upādāya, kataṁ vo taṁ mayā.
root_text.sn35.146:1.20: Etāni, bhikkhave, rukkhamūlāni, etāni suññāgārāni. Jhāyatha, bhikkhave, mā pamādattha; mā pacchāvippaṭisārino ahuvattha. Ayaṁ vo amhākaṁ anusāsanī”ti.

translation_text.sn35.146:1.1: “Mendicants, I will teach you old deeds, new deeds, the cessation of deeds, and the practice that leads to the cessation of deeds.
translation_text.sn35.146:1.2: Listen and apply your mind well, I will speak. …
translation_text.sn35.146:1.4: The eye is old deeds. It should be seen as produced by choices and intentions, as something to be felt.
translation_text.sn35.146:1.6: mind is old deeds. It should be seen as produced by choices and intentions, as something to be felt.
translation_text.sn35.146:1.9: The deeds you currently perform by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.sn35.146:1.11: And what is the cessation of deeds?
translation_text.sn35.146:1.12: When you experience freedom due to the cessation of deeds by body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.sn35.146:1.13: This is called the cessation of deeds.
translation_text.sn35.146:1.14: And what’s the practice that leads to the cessation of deeds?
translation_text.sn35.146:1.15: It is simply this noble eightfold path, that is:
translation_text.sn35.146:1.16: right view, right thought, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, and right immersion.
translation_text.sn35.146:1.17: This is called the practice that leads to the cessation of deeds.
translation_text.sn35.146:1.18: So, mendicants, I’ve taught you old deeds, new deeds, the cessation of deeds, and the practice that leads to the cessation of deeds.
translation_text.sn35.146:1.19: Out of sympathy, I’ve done what a teacher should do who wants what’s best for their disciples.
translation_text.sn35.146:1.20: Here are these roots of trees, and here are these empty huts. Practice absorption, mendicants! Don’t be negligent! Don’t regret it later! This is my instruction to you.”
